mathematical_definition: Sparse convolution noise: a Poisson-distributed set of Gabor kernels (oriented sinusoid windowed by a Gaussian envelope) is scattered over the plane. The response at any point is the sum of contributions from kernels within a finite support radius. Based on Lagae et al. 2009.
value_range: approx. [-1, 1] before remapping (variance depends on kernel density)
art_failure_modes: At low bandwidth, kernels become very large and the field looks like a single sinusoid wave — reduce bandwidth or increase kernel density.
At very high frequency the output saturates to near-uniform noise; keep freq below 8.0 for readable orientation.
The approximation uses a fixed number of kernel taps per cell; low impulse density can show visible Poisson gaps — raise impulse_density.
Without per-octave rotation, FBM of gabor noise locks orientation and looks artificial — vary angle per octave.

complexity_notes.notes: Evaluates multiple kernel contributions per fragment (impulse_density / cell^2 on average). More expensive than Perlin; cache-friendly because all computation is ALU-bound.
